bullets.text: Japan is on high alert for a potentially 'huge' second earthquake after issuing a tsunami warning, with the meteorological agency warning a stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.
More than 200 individuals, including a 14-year-old girl, were rescued from an IS-linked Allied Democratic Forces camp in the Democratic Republic of Congo by the Ugandan army.
An Iranian American woman has been arrested in Los Angeles on suspicion of trafficking weapons to Africa on behalf of the Iranian government, according to a federal prosecutor.
Fears are rising over the potential collapse of a ceasefire between Iran and the US, with a 'yawning gulf' reported between the two nations as the truce nears its end.

Iran has sent mixed signals regarding peace talks, with an official stating positive review of negotiations after earlier reports of no plans for a new round of discussions; this comes as the US has seized a ship.
US-Iran negotiations are in doubt as US negotiators prepare for peace talks in Pakistan, while Australia's Treasurer warns the national economy is 'hostage' to the Iran conflict.

US stocks declined and emerging market currencies traded mixed amid uncertainty over a potential extension of the ceasefire with Iran, following President Trump's statement that an extension is 'highly unlikely' if no deal is reached.
Gold prices fell due to renewed inflation risks stemming from an energy-supply shock in the Middle East and doubts surrounding peace talks to end the Iran war.
US oil refiners are experiencing a windfall from the Iran conflict, benefiting from soaring fuel prices and access to cheap North American crude oil.
